response.justification: Bogdan's background is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js web and full-stack development, with only a brief and dated C++ stint at National Instruments (7 months, 2014–2015) and a thesis project. While he has genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, that work was built on Node.js and D, not C++ or Rust. There is no evidence of routing tile pipelines, navigation data systems, or the kind of systems-level C++ work this role demands. His distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby is a partial positive but insufficient to offset the core skill mismatch.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain knowledge via GISCollective and Green Map Association projects spanning several years
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP req/s) and low-latency design at Dunnhumby
Strong Linux familiarity and consistent application of TDD and quality-engineering practices across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~7 months at NI, 2014–2015) and entirely absent from his skills section; no Rust experience mentioned
No routing tile generation, navigation pipeline, or map data transformation experience despite geospatial adjacent work
Career trajectory is moving further from systems programming — recent roles are TypeScript/Node.js SaaS and AI tooling, with an ongoing career gap
